summary: Full backend + frontend end-to-end testing of OpenJATS Editor. 27/27 backend pytest pass (including live Crossref + ORCID lookups). Frontend e2e Playwright run covered dashboard, filters, theme toggle, article create→editor navigation, metadata fill+save (autosave indicator transitions to 'Saved HH:MM:SS'), authors with fullname auto-fill, ORCID lookup auto-fill (Josiah Carberry), IMRAD 11-section editor with toolbar insertions, references manager (add, Crossref DOI lookup filled real Nature paper title, RIS import grew refs from 1→2), JATS/PKP/Crossref XML tabs, PDF preview, validation panel (3 warnings shown), export menu (all 5 items), templates CRUD. No blocking issues.

ui_bugs.issue: tool-heading (and likely tool-subheading) inserts raw markdown tokens like '##' that are not parsed when rendered in PDF Preview, so the PDF shows the literal '##' characters (e.g., 'This is the introduction text.##'). Either parse markdown when rendering PDF preview, or use JATS/HTML semantic tags instead of markdown-style insertions.

issues: Filter inputs (Volume/Issue/Year/Search) use placeholder text only — no visible labels above the inputs, could hurt accessibility. Otherwise contrast/spacing look good.

action_items: [Optional/Medium] Decide on a markup convention for the IMRAD toolbar. Currently tool-heading inserts '## ' (markdown) which then leaks unrendered into the PDF preview. Either (a) render markdown in PDFPreview, or (b) insert HTML/JATS tags (e.g., <sec><title>…</title></sec>) and render accordingly.
critical_code_review_comments: models.py / server.py: MongoDB _id correctly excluded via projection {'_id': 0} everywhere — good.
server.py: Uses os.environ['MONGO_URL'] and os.environ['DB_NAME'] (no defaults) — good fail-fast.

server.py: External Crossref/ORCID lookups are blocking (`requests` library) inside async endpoints; under load they will block the event loop. Consider moving to httpx.AsyncClient or run_in_threadpool for production. Not a functional bug; works fine for single-editor mode.

server.py: PUT /articles/{id} fully replaces the document — no protection against client overwriting fields that might have been updated by autosave. Acceptable for single-editor mode but worth documenting.
Frontend uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly. data-testid coverage is excellent across all components.
validators.py / jats_generator.py / docx_generator.py work as advertised based on endpoint outputs.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all data-testids resolved, all primary flows successful; one rendering nit on toolbar markdown leaking into PDF)

test_credentials: None — single-editor mode, no auth (confirmed in /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own ephemeral 'TEST_*' prefixed articles and templates and clean them up. A few 'Untitled Article' rows from prior runs remain on the dashboard.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_openjats_api.py is a complete regression suite (articles CRUD, validation, JATS/PKP/Crossref/DOCX, references import/export/format, templates CRUD, external lookups). External Crossref/ORCID worked in this environment — test_lookup_crossref/orcid use pytest.skip() on 502/504. Frontend Playwright run covered the full happy path; only outstanding observation is the IMRAD toolbar inserts raw '##' which is not parsed by PDFPreview.
rca of the issue: IMRAD toolbar 'tool-heading' inserts the literal string '## ' (or similar markdown) into the textarea-introduction; PDFPreview renders the section bodies verbatim without a markdown parser, so the '##' tokens leak into the final layout. Fix: either replace toolbar inserters with semantic markup that PDFPreview already understands, or wire react-markdown / remark into PDFPreview for the section bodies. Reproduction: Editor → IMRAD → Introduction → type text → click 'Heading' tool → switch to PDF Preview → see '##' rendered literally.
